2606.15291.title: A Formal Framework for Declarative Agentic AI in Business Process Analysis
2606.15291.paper_summary: Agentic AI opens new opportunities for automating Business Process (BP), enabling autonomous decision-making and dynamic adaptation. However, realising this potential requires BP entities and their interactions to be defined with formal precision. This paper presents a formal framework for Agentic BP analysis through the AGO methodology. AGO captures the modelling perspective in terms of who is acting (Agents), why it is carried out (Goals), and what the relevant entities are (Objects). Grounded in set theory and mathematical logic, we formally define the AGO entity types and their interactions, organising all definitions into a BP Knowledge Base (BPKB). The resulting BPKB supports structured querying, incremental updates, and automatic generation of BP workflows, while ensuring soundness and completeness of the derived paths.
2606.15291.paper_summary_zh: 代理人工智慧為自動化商業流程（BP）開啟了新的機會，使自主決策和動態適應成為可能。<lb/>然而，實現這一潛力需要以正式的精確度定義BP實體及其互動。<lb/>本文通過AGO方法論提出了一個代理BP分析的正式框架。<lb/>AGO從行動者（代理人）、執行原因（目標）和相關實體（對象）的角度捕捉建模視角。<lb/>基於集合論和數學邏輯，我們正式定義了AGO實體類型及其互動，並將所有定義組織到一個BP知識庫（BPKB）中。<lb/>所得到的BPKB支持結構化查詢、增量更新和BP工作流程的自動生成，同時確保所推導路徑的健全性和完整性。
2606.15291.author: Mohammad Azarijafari et.al.
2606.15291.authors: Mohammad Azarijafari, Luisa Mich, Michele Missikoff
